name: Coat of Padarn
description: The Coat of Padarn, also known as the Mantle of Padarn Beisrudd (Padarn of the Scarlet Robe), is a prominent artifact associated with Saint Padarn, a 6th-century Welsh bishop and founder of Llanbadarn Fawr. This magnificent coat is not merely a garment but a symbol of Padarn's authority, sanctity, and divine favor. It is often described as being of exceptional craftsmanship and beauty, possibly adorned with precious stones or imbued with protective enchantments. The coat's origins are somewhat shrouded in legend, but it is believed to have been bestowed upon Padarn as a sign of his holy office or perhaps even miraculously created. The Coat of Padarn is mentioned in various Welsh traditions and tales, often linked to the establishment and legitimacy of Llanbadarn Fawr as a significant religious center. It represents the spiritual power and influence that Padarn wielded, and its presence would have been a powerful symbol for his followers. The loss or disappearance of the coat could be interpreted as a sign of decline or a challenge to Padarn's legacy. While specific powers are not always explicitly detailed, the coat's association with a revered saint suggests that it might possess blessings of protection, healing, or divine guidance. The Coat of Padarn remains a potent symbol of Welsh religious history and the enduring influence of Saint Padarn.
subtitle: Symbol of Padarn's sanctity and power
material: Unknown, possibly wool or linen, possibly embellished with jewels.
origin: Gifted to or miraculously created for Saint Padarn.

keyMyths.title: Padarn's Founding of Llanbadarn Fawr
keyMyths.description: The coat is often associated with Padarn's establishment of Llanbadarn Fawr, symbolizing his authority and divine approval. Its presence would have legitimized his position and attracted followers to the new religious center.
keyMyths.source: Various Welsh hagiographies and local traditions.

keyMyths.title: The Coat as a Symbol of Office
keyMyths.description: The Coat of Padarn served as a visual representation of Padarn's role as a bishop and his connection to the divine. It was a tangible symbol of his spiritual power and influence within the community.
keyMyths.source: Interpretations of Welsh religious history and folklore.

sources.author: Peter Bartrum
sources.description: Provides genealogical information related to Saint Padarn and his historical context.
sources.text: Provides genealogical information related to Saint Padarn and his historical context.

sources.author: Nora K. Chadwick
sources.work: The Age of the Saints in the Early Celtic Church
sources.description: Discusses the historical and cultural context of saints like Padarn in early Wales.
sources.text: Discusses the historical and cultural context of saints like Padarn in early Wales.
